header.tagline: Your smart guide to choosing whether to rent or buy in Norway.

inputs.years: Years You Plan to Live There

tooltips.monthlyRent: Your current monthly rent. Used as the baseline cost for the renting scenario.
tooltips.rentIncrease: How much your landlord raises the rent each year. Norwegian average is around 3–5%.
tooltips.purchasePrice: The asking price of the home you want to buy.
tooltips.downPayment: Auto-set to 15% of the purchase price. Norwegian law requires a minimum 15% down payment (10% for first-time buyers under 34).
tooltips.mortgageRate: The annual interest rate on your mortgage. Check with your bank for a current offer.
tooltips.loanTermYears: How many years until your mortgage is fully paid off. 25–30 years is most common in Norway.
tooltips.monthlyHoaFee: Monthly joint costs in a shared housing cooperative — includes maintenance, insurance, and management fees.
tooltips.stampDuty: Automatically set to 2.5% of the purchase price. Set to 0 for new builds or housing cooperatives (borettslag).
tooltips.otherClosingCosts: Other one-time costs at purchase: tinglysning (deed + mortgage registration), bank setup fees, etc. Typically 5,000–15,000 kr.
tooltips.brokerSellingFee: A typical broker fee in Norway is around 100,000 kr or 1–3% of the sale price.
tooltips.sharedDebt: Shared debt in a borettslag that transfers with the unit. Affects total cost and tax deductions.
tooltips.municipalFees: Annual municipal fees covering water, sewage, and waste collection. Typically 10,000–20,000 kr/year.
tooltips.renovationPct: Annual maintenance budget as a percentage of the home's value. A common rule of thumb is 0.5–1% per year.
tooltips.homeInsurance: Annual insurance for the building structure. Usually 5,000–15,000 kr/year depending on size.
tooltips.propertyTax: Property tax charged by some Norwegian municipalities. Many municipalities charge 0%. Check your local rate.
tooltips.hoaFeeIncrease: How much the HOA / shared costs rise each year. Boards typically adjust annually in line with costs.
tooltips.years: How long you plan to stay. The longer you own, the more the upfront buying costs are spread out.
tooltips.appreciationRate: Norwegian average home price growth is approximately 2–4% per year.
tooltips.rentGrowth: How fast rents rise in your area. Used to project renting costs over time.
tooltips.investmentReturn: Expected annual return if you invest the down payment and monthly savings instead of buying. Stocks historically ~5–7%.
tooltips.contentsInsurance: Contents insurance covering your belongings. Typically 1,500–3,000 kr/year as a renter.
tooltips.electricity: Annual electricity and heating costs. Varies widely — roughly 15,000–30,000 kr/year for an apartment.
tooltips.internet: Annual broadband cost. Typically around 4,000–6,000 kr/year in Norway.
tooltips.parking: Monthly parking costs if applicable. Leave at 0 if included in rent or not relevant.
tooltips.interestDeduction: In Norway, you can deduct 22% of your mortgage interest from taxable income.
tooltips.inflation: General price growth used to convert future amounts to today's money. Norwegian long-run target is 2%.
tooltips.wageGrowth: Expected annual growth in your income. Used to assess affordability over time.

recommendation.buyDesc: Based on your inputs, buying a home leaves you {{amount}} better off after {{years}} years compared to renting and investing.
recommendation.rentDesc: Based on your inputs, renting and investing the difference leaves you {{amount}} better off after {{years}} years compared to buying.
recommendation.disclaimer: This is an estimate based on your inputs and assumptions about future market conditions. Always consult a qualified financial advisor before making major financial decisions.
